learner_goal: I want to explain why vaccines work.
launch_attempt: Vaccines safely preview an antigen so the body responds faster later.
cold_attempt: A vaccine presents antigen safely and leaves memory cells for later exposure.
repair_dialogue_turns: Memory cells keep the pattern from the safe preview, so later exposure triggers a faster response.
gap_attempt: The repaired link is that memory cells connect safe first exposure to a faster later response.
spaced_attempt: Vaccination creates memory cells after safe antigen exposure, and those memory cells drive a faster response during later infection.
